title: The MACU Report — Episode 11 — Seeding
notes: WEEK 2 opener (Mon) — Road to the Crater Bowl. Fresh reset: NO Unit 7 / moon / Signal. Spine: Ron's face-painted devotion to the Sector Nine Slags (roster 2 -> 1 living players) heading to Friday's Crater Bowl; Walter the deadpan straight man. NEW VOICE: GARY = Newman profile bebcfa40 (INTERCOM-ONLY; no on-camera shots — all Gary cues are intercom/door/shadow b-roll). NEW: Coach Bodhi = Snoop (31649b70), serene guru. Writers'-room punch-up applied (v3 script): whistleblower pays off in signoff (transfers-to-affiliate-roster -> Ron 'just paperwork'); house-bet made Monday-true; Gary's lockout forces the weather toss; Radbites got an absurd flavor specific; Carl undercuts Ron ('one of em's limping'); 3-thread signoff collision. NEW GRAPHICS (Hyperframes, Max to render+copy to titles/): the_crater_bowl (comp 'crater-bowl-card'), scoreboard_2 + scoreboard_1 (comp 'crater-bowl-scoreboard-card', two states), macu_weather (REUSE ep7 weather_card). NO hold cues this ep. OmniVoice /generate takes multipart form-data (-F), NOT JSON (per Max — earlier failure was content-type).

comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w (watermark-free). 384x384x24f locked - do not bump resolution.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. No hold cues this episode. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Apply jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx one-shots, burn subs -> final/ep11.mp4.

title_assets.the_crater_bowl: episodes/ep11/titles/the_crater_bowl.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes comp 'crater-bowl-card'; Max: render + copy here)
title_assets.scoreboard_2: episodes/ep11/titles/scoreboard_2.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes 'crater-bowl-scoreboard-card', state: SLAGS 2 / GLOWBOYS 40 / PLAYERS REMAINING)
title_assets.scoreboard_1: episodes/ep11/titles/scoreboard_1.mp4 (NEW — same comp, state: SLAGS 1 / PLAYERS REMAINING — the in-ep heighten 2->1)
title_assets.macu_weather: episodes/ep11/titles/macu_weather.mp4 (REUSE — copy episodes/ep7/titles/weather_card.mp4 here, or auto-resolves from shared assets/titles)

beds.name: sports
beds.note: Hotter big-band bed under the live game action (Crater Carl play-by-play). Loop to cover.

music.note: Intro + a hotter sports bed under the live game + outro. Rest plays dry.

sfx.note: One-shots mixed at each cue's start (VO stays full). SYNTH (Max, ffmpeg into assets/sfx/): intercom_crackle.wav (short static burst — Gary's intercom signature). ACQUIRE (Max, CC0 freesound, exact filenames): whistle_blow.wav, impact_thud.wav, crowd_murmur.wav, door_thud.wav. Missing file -> skip gracefully.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own from font/fontsize.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU — and what an evening! The Sector Nine Slags are going to the Crater Bowl!

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: They are. To clarify: the Slags advanced because the other three teams could not field eleven living players between them.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's called peaking at the right time, Walter. That's called destiny.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: Walter! Waaalter. It's Gary. From the weekend. Big game, huh, buddy? You, me — we carpool to the Bowl. I'll drive. I'll bring the cooler. Walter? Is this thing on?

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: It's on, Gary. To the field.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We go live to the Big Hole, where superfan correspondent Crater Carl is standing by.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Thanks, Walter! The Slags have two healthy players and both of them showed up — that's a hundred percent attendance, baby!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Two committed athletes beat forty cowards, Carl!

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: You said it, Ron! Small roster, big heart — one of 'em's limping, but that's playoff grit! — and a head coach who has stopped blinking entirely!

cues.speaker: RADBITES
cues.vo: In the twilight after the AI Wars of Twenty Twenty-Eight, a new sustenance was born from the ashes. From the irradiated wastelands — from the creatures the wastelands changed — we make food. We call it Radbites.

cues.speaker: RADBITES
cues.vo: Now in three flavors: Marsh, Burrow, and Original. Arm yourself for the stormy days ahead. Embrace the inevitable. Radbites. Call now — five five five, one two three four — for a free, no-obligation introduction to the new taste of survival. One signal is all it takes.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Not everyone shares the excitement. We're joined by a former Slag.

cues.speaker: WHISTLEBLOWER
cues.vo: Yeah. Hi. I played three games. I'm the only one who — look, the reason they never lose a player is they don't report the —

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: We'll take a quick break!

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Before the break ran long, the official Crater Bowl line: the Glowboys are forty-point favorites, and the over-under on surviving players is one and a half.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Put my house on the Slags. The whole house. And my chair.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron, it is Monday.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: And I'll do it again Tuesday. That's called consistency.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Let's go back down to the Big Hole, where there is — against medical advice — a game in progress.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: We're live, and Sector Nine has the ball — or a head, hard to tell from here — number two is running, he's past midfield, past the wall, past the medic, the medic did not even try to stop him —

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Oh. Oh, that is a hit. That is a tritanium-grade hit — he is down, and the crowd is going — somewhere — they are filing for the exits, folks, and frankly, that is the smart play!

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Slags recover the ball! And that brings Sector Nine's active roster to — let me check — one. One Slag. What a game! What a season!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: For perspective, let's hear from the Slags' legendary coach and spiritual advisor — Coach Bodhi.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: You can't lose a player, man. You relocate his energy. Number eleven didn't pass away. He diversified. He's in the wind now. He's in the soil. He's in the snacks.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: We don't grieve in Sector Nine. We compost. That's the system. That's the heart.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's the heart I keep telling you about, Walter.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: He composted a man, Ron.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: Ron! Ronald! It's Gary. I put a little somethin' on the Slags too — solidarity. Also, I'm outside. The door's locked. Is it always locked? Walter? ...Buddy?

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: That'll be the wind. Friday's forecast.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ashfall, a chance of structural collapse, and a condition rating of mild. Perfect Crater Ball weather.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. Friday: the Crater Bowl. Two Slags, forty Glowboys, and all the heart in the world. I have never been more certain of anything in my life.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: The league has clarified that any Slags lost on Friday will not be recorded as losses — but as transfers to an affiliate roster.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: See? It's just paperwork. That's the heart, Walter.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: I can see you, fellas. The little red light's on.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: He has been certain since breakfast. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: ...Hello? ...Fellas? Hello.
